§ The Gemara asks: From where do we derive that the Persians descend from Japheth? The Gemara answers: As it is written: “The sons of Japheth were Gomer and Magog and Madai and Javan and Tuval and Meshech and Tiras” (Genesis 10:2). The Gemara explains: Gomer, that is Germamya; Magog, that is Kandiya; Madai, that is Macedonia; Javan, in accordance with its plain meaning, Greece; Tuval, that is the…

The sons of Japheth, Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Thubal, and Meshek, and Thiras. And the names of their provinces, Afriki, and Germania, and Medi, and Makadonia, and Iatinia, and Asia, and Tharki.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Radak on I Chronicles 1:7:1
matches.x: The sons of Javan. The text leaves out Magog, Madai, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras—as it does in Genesis (10:3–5)—in order to be brief, because their progenies do not amount to anything important. As for Gomer and Javan, tbey might have been the heads of the families that descended from Japheth.
The sons of Yafes were: Gomer, Magog, Modai, Yavan, Tuval, Meshech, and Tiras.

Cush produced [a son] Nimrod. He began to be [mighty and] powerful on earth.

Rabbi Chakhinai said: Nimrod was a mighty hero, as it is said, "And Cush begat Nimrod, who began to be a mighty one in the earth" (Gen. 10:8). Rabbi Jehudah said: The coats which the Holy One, blessed be He, made for Adam and his wife, were with Noah in the ark, and when they went forth from the ark, || Ham, the son of Noah, brought them forth with him, and gave them as an inheritance to Nimrod.…
And Kush begat Nimrod: he began to be mighty in sin, and to rebel before the Lord in the earth.

matches.lb: Second Temple: The Midrash of Philo 10:8:1
matches.x: Why was Chus the father of Nimrod, who began to be a giant and a hunter before the Lord: on which account they said, "Like Nimrod the mighty hunter before the Lord?" (Genesis 10:8).
He was a mighty hunter [mighty and powerful] before Adonoy. It is therefore said, Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter [mighty and powerful] before Adonoy.

He was a mighty hunter before the Lord; Nimrod was considered a hero not only in his immediate surroundings, but also on a global scale. Therefore it is said over the course of the generations, as a kind of popular folk saying: Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the Lord. When people speak of an outstanding hunter, a great hero, or a mighty king, they compare him to Nimrod.

THE BROTHER OF JAPHETH THE ELDER. It is the way of Scripture to record a younger brother beside the oldest of his brothers, and not beside a younger one than himself.328See above, 6:10, that Ramban’s opinion is that Noah’s children were born in this order: Japheth, Shem, and Ham. Ramban thus says here that it is customary for Scripture to record a second son (Shem) beside the oldest (Japheth)…

matches.x: THESE ARE THE SONS OF HAM, AFTER THEIR FAMILIES, AFTER THEIR TONGUES. Scripture says the same concerning the children of Japheth16Gen. 10:5. and the children of Shem.17Gen. 10:31.

These are the sons of Shem, as they gradually spread: After their families, clans and tribes; after their tongues, as each family developed a language of its own; in their lands, since afterward they spread out over a geographical expanse; and after their nations, for each became a nation in its own right.
These are the sons of Sheim, according to their families and their languages, in their lands and in their nations.

These are the sons of Shem, as they gradually spread: After their families, clans and tribes; after their tongues, as each family developed a language of its own; in their lands, since afterward they spread out over a geographical expanse; and after their nations, for each became a nation in its own right.
These are the sons of Sheim, according to their families and their languages, in their lands and in their nations.
These are the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands, in their nations.
ללשנתם בארצתם AFTER THEIR TONGUES, IN THEIR LANDS — Although these were of different tongues and lands they are nevertheless all sons of Ham.

These are the families of the sons of Noah, in their legacy, in their nations; and from these the nations were dispersed on the earth after the flood, over the course of the generations.
These are the families of the sons of Noach, according to their descendants and in their nations. From these the nations were separated on the earth after the Flood.
